observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant
complete the necessary procedures of the one-time cleansing and render
appropriate reports to establish the correct balances of the PPE items that
shall be reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management send demand letters to employees who
have not liquidated their cash advances. Additionally, Management has to
implement measures to ensure the immediate liquidation of long-overdue
cash advances by withholding or suspending the salaries or any amount due
to the concerned officials and employees for non-compliance, in accordance
with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445.

To address the discrepancies between the cash balances in accounting and
treasury records, it is recommended that management implement a more
structured and frequent reconciliation process between the two
departments. It is also important to conduct a thorough internal audit to
identify any underlying issues or weaknesses in internal controls, which
could be contributing to these discrepancies.

We reiterate our recommendation that management institutionalize a formal
and regular reconciliation mechanism between the two departments and
conduct a thorough internal audit to uncover the root causes and any internal
control weaknesses. Additionally, Management must ensure the full
cooperation and accountability of all involved personnel—especially the
former accountable officer—in the timely resolution of outstanding
discrepancies and the restoration of accurate and reliable financial records.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer update her
cashbooks daily and periodically reconcile her records with the Municipal
Accountant in order to ensure that recorded transactions are complete,
reliable, accurate and timely.

Furthermore, we reiterate the necessity of conducting regular and properly
documented reconciliations with the Municipal Accountant, as this was a key
concern highlighted in previous audit findings and remains essential to accurate
and timely financial reporting.

We recommended and management agreed to require its suppliers /
contractors to issue Invoices in the procurement of goods and services
pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 7-2024 dated March 22, 2024, and
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that management furnish us a written explanation on the
cited discrepancies and ensure that future transactions properly undergo
the procurement process.

It is recommended that the HRMO enforce stricter monitoring and
compliance with attendance policies. A comprehensive review of employees’
attendance records should be conducted, and appropriate actions should be
taken against those found violating attendance rules.

We recommended and management agreed to strive for the timely and full
implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective
of the fund as well as the desired benefits.

It is recommended that management hire additional qualified accounting
personnel, including filling the vacant Admin Assistant III / Senior
Bookkeeper position. Additionally, recruiting Accounting Clerks or
Bookkeepers would help distribute the workload more effectively and
ensure timely completion of tasks. Training existing staff in accounting
functions could also enhance their capacity to assist with technical tasks,
improving overall efficiency and compliance with deadlines.

We recommended that management prioritize filling the vacant positions,
either by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving
staff members, in order to improve service delivery, ensure the full
implementation of the agency's programs, plans, and activities, and provide
equal job opportunities for the community to benefit from.

It is recommended that management provide a written explanation
regarding the noted irregularities.

Additionally, management have to strengthen its internal controls to ensure
that all documents contain the required attachments and signatures. This can be
accomplished by implementing a more thorough review process, establishing
clear guidelines for document handling, and enhancing monitoring to identify
inconsistencies and prevent errors or fraudulent activities.

It is recommended that the LGU launch a comprehensive information
campaign to actively inform taxpayers about the ongoing tax amnesty
program, its benefits, and the available payment options. This campaign
should utilize various platforms, including social media, local media outlets,
public announcements, and community outreach, to ensure maximum
reach. Additionally, the LGU should provide clear and easily accessible
guidance on tax obligations through workshops, seminars, and online
resources, while also strengthening internal controls to ensure the smooth
implementation of the program and efficient collection of taxes.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to allow the timely booking up of the RPT and SET Receivables.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant recognize and properly record the discounts on RPT and SET collections to present accurately the balances of the affected tax receivable and income accounts.  

Finally, it is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper year-end balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommend that management fast track the establishment of an LDRRMO strictly in accordance with Section 12 of R.A. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, and appoint qualified personnel and staff who will adequately and competently perform the functions of the office mandated by law.

We recommend that cash advances be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. The concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate  settlement of the same otherwise the necessary sanction for its settlement such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced. Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to request write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefits of the constituents. Otherwise, replace these with positions/items that are necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

We recommend that management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set aside enough time for the reconciliation of these accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the affected accounts and effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation.

We recommend that management make use of the free accounting system provided the Commission on Audit through compliance of the requirement for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.

We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱4,000.00 take home pay per month for them to maintain self-respect and dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee net pay fall below ₱4,000.00.

We recommend that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the Treasury Department produce the source documents of the above transactions and submit them to the Accounting Department which shall make the following Journal Entry:

 To recognize unrecorded collections.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to exert all efforts in collecting the receivable accounts, if unsuccessful after all efforts have been exhausted, request for write-off/ adjustment of the receivable accounts with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan, duly supported with documents required under Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that:
Management conduct physical inventory of all its PPE pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 and submit the RPCPPE.
Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to correct the misstatement in the affected accounts.
Henceforth, ensure that the costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement and construction of infrastructure assets be capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment in accordance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended that management make representations on the release and turn-over of these projects to the barangays who are responsible for the proper operation and maintenance of the structures.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to drop the cost of the assets that are not owned/controlled by the municipality from its books after due notice on the turn-over in accordance with paragraph 7 of the International  Public  Sector  Accounting  Standard (IPSAS) 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the transactions in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer shall settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amount for its intended purpose.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the appropriate journal entry to re-classify the above subject accounts to its appropriate account titles in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are presented fairly.
We recommended and management agreed to review their transaction recording procedures concerning the collection and remittance of RPT and SET and implement any necessary adjustments accordingly.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure the submission of the Confidential Fund cash advance vouchers together with complete supporting documents to the COA Audit Team Leader within seven days after the release of the checks in accordance with Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-DND-Governance Commission for GOCCs JMC No. 2015-01.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Submit to the audit team the lacking documentary requirements relative to the release of the cash advance for confidential fund.
Submit to the COA-ICFAU the liquidation documents pursuant to Section 6.2 of COA-DBM-DILG-DND-Governance Commission for GOCCs JMC No. 2015-01. A copy of the transmittal letter duly received by ICFAU shall be furnished to the Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare a Memorandum of Agreement that delineates, among other terms, the respective roles, responsibilities and obligations of the parties every time there is a transfer of funds to barangays and other implementing agencies.

In view hereof, we recommended and management agreed to submit the lacking proof that 70% of all planned programs/activities/projects have been implemented and completed in accordance with the targets for CY 2023, pursuant to Item 5.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the municipal officials responsible for the implementation of the LDRRM plans to exert utmost efforts in ensuring that the planned projects, programs and activities are fully implemented and that funds are efficiently utilized in accordance with existing regulations in order to fully realize the benefits thereof.

We recommended that:
The Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Office and other implementing offices to review the composition of the continuing 20% Development Fund, identify the savings of completed projects and those which were no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC, re-appropriate to current/existing priority development projects of the 20% DF.
The MPDO and Municipal Engineer’s Office coordinate and strategize to optimally utilize the 20% DF; and regularly monitor the status of utilization and implementation of PPAs.

The Head of Agency shall create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency.

Prepare and submit the PIP for approval to the Head of the Agency to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;

Submit a Copy of the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; and

Prepare and submit a consolidated Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

We recommend that management prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to support the release and use of its LDRRM funds including the unexpended balances from previous years.

Also, it is recommended that management ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances of the 30% QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the LDRRMF be transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liabilities – DRRM and program the same for disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMF Investment Plan within the next five years. Management should likewise ensure that any unutilized amount after the five-year period be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and shall be made available for other social services as may be enacted by the local sanggunian.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the concerned municipal officials, maximize the use of its 20% Development Fund by dedicating it to relevant and responsive development projects, programs and activities that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommend that management ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are effectively controlled and properly accounted for by complying with the rules and regulations prescribed in COA Circular No.77-61 especially on the use of approved Requisition and Issue Voucher or its equivalent, so as to avoid or at least minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommend that management, through its LDRRMO, prepare the LDRRMFIP annually in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC-DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 to support the release and use of its LDRRMF including the unexpended funds in the preceding years.

We recommend that management demand from the concerned barangays the immediate settlement of their unliquidated fund transfers, and render compliance with Item 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in relation to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of funds transferred to implementing agencies.

We recommend that management utilize the LDRRMF only for expenditures which are directly related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery in adherence to Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 and Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommend that management require the concerned job orders personnel to prepare and submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that services are actually rendered in accordance with their job order contract and are truly necessary to meet the service requirements of the LGU.

We likewise recommend that management ensure that payments of salaries, and wages are supported with complete and duly-accomplished supporting documents prescribed under existing rules and regulations.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository banks of the LGU and record reconciling items on a timely basis for an accurate and correct Cash In Bank balance reflected in the books of accounts. Likewise, prompt adjustments of said reconciling items will ensure the early detection of discrepancies and its ultimate adjustments in the books can be facilitated.

 For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.

For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and which require placement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor/Accountant for presentation to the Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to properly prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and submit them to the Accounting Department daily to avoid loss of documents and allow the Accounting Department to record transactions daily in accordance with the New Government Accounting System Manual.

We recommend that the recipients refund the award paid by the municipality. 

Henceforth stop the granting of similar benefits to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the municipal accountant to record as receivables the related income for unpaid market fees with probability of collection as of December 31, 2019, as follows:

Account Receivables           
               ₱28,300.00
Receipt from Market Operation      ₱28,300.00
        To record delinquent rental fees of stall occupants for CY2019 and below.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Collections and Deposits daily to facilitate prompt recording and preparation and submission of periodic financial statements.  We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to be mindful of the deadlines of the financial reports in order to set priorities in the report preparation.

We recommend that management:

Request the Municipal Mayor who is on leave of absence for the whereabouts of the reported missing disbursement vouchers and require the immediate submission of the same.

The municipal treasurer and accountant should coordinate, make a thorough search and trace in the logbook for the concerned personnel who had the last handled the disbursement vouchers to pinpoint responsibility.  The municipal treasurer should closely monitor the trail of the disbursement vouchers being the person accountable of the said documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort on the reconciliation of these accounts to produce reliable financial statements, at the same time, bracing reliance and sincerity of the Accounting Department in performing its function.  Further, we highly recommend that the Municipal Mayor give due regard on hiring additional employee/s to focus on the reconciliation of prior period unreconciled beginning balances in order to present reliable balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the abnormal balances, the history thereof, and the consequences relative to the implementation of the computerization and change of accountants that resulted to these abnormal balances and adjust immediately recent accounting errors so these abnormal balances will not accumulate.
We recommend that management consider revising/updating the rates in the Local Revenue Code in compliance with RA 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide more funds for its operations.

We recommend that management:

Submit explanation/justification why public bidding was not conducted and instead shopping was pursued.  Henceforth, the LGU should strictly comply with Section 52 of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 168 (c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume

Require the immediate submission of complete and original documents to support all claims as follows:

a.	Original copies of purchase requests and purchase orders, duly numbered, dated and the specific purpose indicated.

b.	Delivery Receipts duly filled up by the supplier as to the detailed specifications particularly the Serial Numbers of the items delivered.

c.	Inspection and acceptance report by the agency personnel indicating the date of acceptance/inspection and the detailed specification of the items delivered specifically the Serial Numbers of the Goulds Submersible Pumps and Franklin Motor units.  Indicate whether items accepted/received were complete or partial and the items delivered were in accordance with specifications and quantities required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate and reconcile the records with Municipal Assessors Office and Municipal General Services Office to ascertain the correctness of the Land account.
Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to record the cost/value assigned by the Inventory and Appraisal Committee based on the market/fair value for properties found at the station.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to validate the credit and debit advice reflected in bank statements and draw journal entry vouchers to record the reconciling items that require adjustment in General Ledger to present the account in the financial statements fairly.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the warranty security required in the procurement of goods; and
Ensure that for future procurements, retain an amount equivalent to at least 1% of the contract price or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount from the suppliers,  to guarantee that that supplier will be liable for any manufacturing defects.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures on Loans Payable account so that the liquidity and solvency of the Municipality could be properly assessed.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed by the municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting, by taking up first the construction materials procured, under the Construction Materials Inventory, upon purchase, in compliance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
Management Comment(s)
During the audit exit conference, the Municipal Accountant commented that they will adhere to the audit recommendations. She also stated that this would result in strict monitoring of materials and supplies used in various projects implemented by the administration.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment reports, and office order/ work arrangements;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds, especially the COVID-19 hazard pay, are supported with complete documentary requirements as provided under Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) of 1445.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted and assured us that they will submit the lacking supporting documents. Also, they assured the Audit Team that they will ensure complete documentary requirements in future transactions.

We recommended that Management:
Require the recipients of the Hazard Pay to refund the excess amount totaling ₱104,032.80; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly adhere to the related guidelines for future claims against government funds.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted and agreed to return or refund the excess amount. Also, they assured the Audit Team that they will adhere to the guidelines set for their future claims.

We recommended that Management:
Require the recipients of the Hazard Pay to refund the excess amount totaling ₱432,000.00; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly adhere to the related guidelines for future claims against government funds.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted and agreed to return or refund the excess amount. Also, they assured the Audit Team that they will adhere to the guidelines set for their future claims.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Health Officer to submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment reports, and office order/ work arrangements;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds, especially the SRA, are supported with complete documentary requirements as provided under Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) of 1445.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted and assured us that they will submit the lacking supporting documents. Also, they assured the Audit Team that they will ensure complete documentary requirements in future transactions.

We recommended that Management require the concerned MHO Employees to return/refund the amount of overpayment. Additionally, in the succeeding payment of SRAs:
Direct the Municipal Health Officer to strictly follow the guidelines set for computation of the benefits (₱5,000.00 per month threshold on SRA) and;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of all claims, especially the SRA, as provided under Item 3.1 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted and agreed to return or refund the amount of overpayment. Also, they assured the Audit Team that they will strictly follow the guidelines set for computation of the benefits and will ensure the accuracy of all claims in future transactions.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper recording of transactions to avoid such errors so that the propriety and validity of the recorded transactions are maintained.
Management Comment(s)
The Management through the Municipal Accountant acknowledged the deficiencies and assured the Audit Team that they will ensure proper recording of transactions to avoid such errors in the future.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Require the end-user to submit the Terms of Reference for future consultancy works before the conduct of procurement;
Update themselves on the government procurement by attending online training, if possible, or equip themselves with the RA 9184 handbook; and
Ensure strict observance of the proper procurement procedures for future procurement of consulting services as required under Section 2 and 53.7 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Management Comment(s)
It was transpired in the exit conference that the Management acknowledged the deficiencies noted. The Chairman of the BAC emphasized that they will adhere to the audit recommendations to ensure strict observance of the proper procurement procedures in the future.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to
ensure that for future transactions, only half or fifty percent of the negotiated price of the amount of land will be paid upon the execution of the deed of sale and the remaining half will be paid upon the transfer of title in the name of the LGU, as provided under Section 6.11 of the IRR of RA 10752, to protect itself from possible loss in times of land disputes;
ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are properly supported with necessary documents before processing the payments; and
Municipal Assessor to
immediately comply with the documentary requirements needed by the BIR to expedite the processing of transfer of title in favor of the Municipality to avoid any future land disputes.
Management Comment(s)
The Management agreed with the audit recommendations and will ensure that on the succeeding transactions, only half or fifty percent of the negotiated price of land will be paid upon execution of the deed of sale and the remaining half will be paid upon transfer of the title in the name of the LGU Cortes.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to:
Discontinue the payment of cash gift earlier than the prescribed period; and
Update themselves on the existing guidelines on the payment of cash gifts and other benefits for Municipal Officials and Employees, particularly DBM Circular No. 2016-4, to ensure proper release of the said benefits.
Management Comment(s)
The Management acknowledged the deficiencies of the early release of cash gifts for CY 2021 and assured the Audit Team that starting CY2022, they will follow the schedule on the release of cash gifts and other benefits based on the DBM Circular.

Recommendation:

Recommendation:
We recommend that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Recommendation:
We recommend that cash advances be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. The concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same otherwise the necessary sanction for its settlement such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced. Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts as required in paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) in order to present an accurate and reliable financial information.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions in COA Circular 2012-002 as follows:
Disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Notes to the Financial Statements and
Revert back to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund any unexpended balances beyond the five (5) years requirement of being in the special trust fund.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management stop the practice of pre-signing checks to prevent unauthorized disbursements.  Instead, we recommend that an alternate signatory be considered so that payment of recurring transactions can be done at any time.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefits of the constituents. Otherwise, replace these with positions/items that are necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved as required in Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.

In view thereof, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be cautious of performing their tasks.  Further, we also encourage the department to assist the Accounting Department in the reconciliation on the reciprocal accounts in order to correct the errors committed.

We recommend that management:
a.  Adopt the perpetual inventory method for the Office Supplies, Drugs and Medicine Supplies and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies by setting up Inventory Accounts.
b.  Coordinate directly with the concerned departments for the submission of duly acknowledged Requisition and Issue Slips for the withdrawal/issuance of supplies and the Monthly Report of the Office Supplies, Drugs and Medicines, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies issued and its supporting documents, in order to establish inventories on hand at year-end.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the affected accounts and effect necessary adjusting entries after reconciliation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain the availability of funds of the above expenditures according to their correct expense classification and to effect the adjustments on the object of expenditure on the Registries of Appropriation and Obligation, as well as on the Obligation Requests.  Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer has to be prudent in the charging of expenditures payables are properly obligated and ensure that budget allocations are properly monitored.

It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace and reconcile these accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments and ensure that all balances reported in the Financial Statements are accurate and reliable.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the entries made in the Government Equity account and provide adjustments to correct the misstatement of the equity and other affected accounts in the current and prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.

We recommend that management make use of the free accounting system provided the Commission on Audit through compliance of the requirement for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that management require the MSWDO to coordinate with the punong barangays and other focal persons to submit a summary of identified beneficiaries and the specific documentary requirement provided by the beneficiary and certified it as to the authenticity of documents to ensure that beneficiaries are properly validated and avoid duplication in the listing.  Henceforth, the MSWDO has to update its records/database of all the low-income and vulnerable sectors of the society to ensure that they are the actual and valid recipients of any cash assistance extended by the government in times of crisis situation such as the Covid-19 pandemic.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to produce and deposit immediately the undeposited collection of ₱463,244.35 and to impose the necessary sanctions for the lapses of the later.

We recommend that management conduct a thorough investigation on the matter and instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to properly maintain check registers where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the check in the check register as a tool of controlling the issuance of the checks and detecting possible irregularities and avoiding possible claim disputes and penalties.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value to ensure compliance with the aforecited regulation and for monitoring of its use and condition.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resources Management Office to implement the “No DTR/Application for Leave, No Salary” Policy to ensure all municipal employees readily submit their respective DTRs and approved Application for Leave at the end of each month.  We also recommend that management monitor the leave credit balances of all elected officials and regular employees to avoid undue payments of salaries at the disadvantage of the government.

We recommend that the municipality properly control and account for their gasoline consumption and use of motor vehicles as provided in COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977, which include among others completing the details in the approved Driver’s Trip Tickets every trip.

In view hereof, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the property custodian to facilitate the issuance and the renewal of all PARs and cancel or replace PARs on property whose custodianship is already transferred to another official or employee.

We recommend the management to submit explanation on the noted deficiencies and submit the required documents to prove the validity, regularity and reasonableness on the utilization of the grant in accordance with DBM LBC 125.

We recommend that management require the department heads of the health services sectors to include a certification on the Purchase Requests for all drugs and medicines needs in accordance with E.O 49 and in observance of item 5 of DBM LBC 125.  Henceforth, the BAC has to ensure that certifications are included in all PRs submitted for procurement of drugs and medicines, otherwise, future similar deficiencies noted will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an entry reclassifying the CIP- Other Infrastructure to Water Supply System and recognize the asset's corresponding depreciation expense as prescribed under the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements, the Property, Plant and Equipment Schedule pursuant to the provisions of the GAM on disclosure of PPE. Also, facilitate the reconciliation with the Report on Physical of Property, Plant and Equipment on the discrepancies noted.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to-
Ensure that only half or fifty-percent of the negotiated price of the amount of land will be paid upon the execution of the deed of sale and the remaining half will be paid upon the transfer of title in the name of the LGU, as provided under Section 243 of GAM for LGUs;
Ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are properly supported with necessary documents before processing the payments; and
Require the Accounting Personnel to attend relevant seminars and update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Land Acquisitions.
Municipal Assessor to-
Request the pertinent documents of the acquired lot from the concerned Office, and expedite the processing of transfer of title in favor of the Municipality to avoid any future land disputes.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided for the utilization of funds granted, especially those with a specific purpose, such as BGCM Funds, to strengthen the LGU's capacity in responding to the currently faced pandemic.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to be meticulous in the acceptance of documentary requirements for all government procurement, and be updated with the guidelines provided by the relevant and competent government offices.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the BAC Secretariat to immediately comply with the GPPB Online Portal's posting requirement for Emergency Procurement under Republic Act 11469 (Bayanihan to Heal as One Act).
Capacitate the BAC Secretariat on the laws, rules and regulations on government procurement;
Require the BAC Secretariat to promote transparency in government procurement by ensuring that the Notices of Award and Purchase Orders for future procurements are posted in accordance with the guidelines prescribed.

We recommended that Management adhere to the procurement process in the implementation of programs and activities to address the COVID-19 provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 01-2020, and discourage the grant of cash advances to disbursing officers for the procurement of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the conditions set by the National Project Coordination Office (NPCO) to expedite the release of the NOL2, and ensure compliance with the IMA, procurement law, and other pertinent rules and regulations in the subproject implementation.

We recommended that Management strengthen its monitoring controls by requiring the MDRRM Office to:
Ensure issuance of Acknowledgment Receipts to all future receipts of donations in-kind to confirm that donations are properly recorded and accounted for;
10.8.2	Ensure that all future issuances of donated items are properly supported with Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any document/record to prove receipt by and distribution to the intended beneficiaries; and
Determine the inventory of remaining undistributed items for recording in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MDRRM Officer to:
Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and
Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
End-user to:
Prepare the Terms of Reference for future consultancy works and submit the same to Bids and Awards Committee before the conduct of procurement.
Bids and Awards Committee to:
Strictly observe the proper procurement procedures on the hiring of highly technical consultants as required under Section 53.7 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Undertake the negotiation and validate the capability of the individual consultant to undertake and fulfill the consultancy work based on the Terms of Reference prepared by the end-user.

We recommended that Management:
Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the    GAA and ensure that an employee's net take-home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take-home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Sec. 49 of RA 11518 (General Appropriations Act of 2021).
Cease from being a withholding agent on loan obligations of employees in institutions that are not among those specified in the applicable GAA provision.
The Management assured the Audit Team that strict monitoring shall be exercised in the implementation of the recommendations.

We recommended that the purchase orders and contracts together with the supporting documents for all procurement be submitted to the Office not later than five (5) working days from the issuance of PO and perfection of contracts for auditorial review and evaluation of the contracts so that any deficiencies noted can be communicated to the management immediately.

We   recommended   that   Management   stop   charging   ineligible   expenditures to LDRRMF and strictly observe the guidelines  set  in  Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2013-01  of  NDRRMC-DBM-DBM  in  the utilization of LDRRMF to ensure optimal utilization.
We recommended that Management observe safety, proper operation and storage of service vehicles and consider the construction of an additional covered garage to prevent its fast deterioration.

We recommended to fast track the implementation of the programs and projects that aimed to reduce poverty and improve the living conditions of the beneficiaries; the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan procurement activities to avoid delayed implementation of projects; and the Local Chief Executive to create the Project Monitoring Committee/Project Inspection Team that would assist, evaluate and monitor the extent of implementation of programs and projects in order to determine the causes of delay in the implementation.

We recommended and  Management  agreed  to  stop  the  practice  of charging to 20% development fund expenditures that do  not  partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures pursuant  to  the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2017-1  of  the  DBM and DILG to ensure optimal utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant validate the recorded receivable accounts and identify those which needs to be written off.  We also recommend the Municipal Mayor to request authority to write-off the receivables based on the verification made by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and in compliance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the above-mentioned disbursement and the concerned officials and employees refund the amount of taxes paid by the municipality in their behalf.  Henceforth, management has to withhold the accurate amount of income taxes to avoid incurrence of similar situations.

We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer record all transactions in the cashbook daily and properly to establish accuracy of cash balances at any given period of time.  We also recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office reconcile their records at least quarterly in order to correct errors in recording.

We, therefore, recommend the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify the validity of the accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to present reliable account balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to set aside enough time for the reconciliation of these accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with authorized depositary bank as often as necessary and to immediately stop the practice of holding large amount of undeposited collections that deprives the municipality and its constituents to its basic services.
We recommend that management consider revising/updating the rates in the Local Revenue Code in compliance with RA 7160 to maximize its taxing power and to provide more funds for its operations.

We recommend that the management exert effort to collect the right amount of fees and penalties in accordance with the Local Revenue Code in order to maximize revenue generating power of the municipality to support its programs, projects and activities.

We recommend that management submit an explanation on the non-imposition of penalties and surcharges on long overdue collectibles. It is also recommended that henceforth, management implement the imposition of surcharges and penalties unpaid fees pursuant to the provisions on the Revised Municipal Revenue Code so as to generate additional income to the municipal funds.

Further, we recommend that the Municipal Government constitute a Cortes DRRMO manned by the existing personnel in accordance with the qualifications set forth under Section 3 of Rule 6, IRR, RA 10121, as follows:
Must be civil service eligible;
Must have a civil defense/DRM experience.
Furthermore, DRRMO, in coordination with concerned national agencies and instrumentalities, perform the functions set for in Section 4, Rule 6, IRR, RA No. 10121 with impartiality, given the emerging challenges brought by disasters of our times.

We, therefore, recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to review the Annual Budget and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to implement programs, projects and activities pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommend that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management intensify the implementation and monitoring of municipal projects in order to deliver good and timely services which the public deserves.

We recommend that the management evaluate the duties and responsibilities of each employees.  No employee shall perform incompatible functions to avoid manipulation of documents or data.
We also recommend that the municipal mayor assign the preparation of the payroll and remittance list to the Human Resource Management Officer and instruct the accounting department to check the documents from the HR to ensure the accuracy of the payments/remittances.

We recommend that management assign a personnel to review and trace the balances and documents pertaining to the Inter-agency Payables.  Henceforth, we recommend that management impose necessary procedures/controls in order to monitor the proper deduction and remittances of the contributions and/or loan payments to the National and Corporate Government Agencies.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the traveling expenditures are made within the prescribed limits as provided under Section 6 of EO 198.
We recommend that management abide by the rules and regulations regarding the timely payment of wages for job order employees and that in no time shall these be given before services are rendered.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to perform the Inventory Count and identify the Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties.  We also recommend that management facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall include in their work plan the performance of complete conduct of physical count in compliance with Section 124 of the New Government Accounting System Manual.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to stop the delivery of checks to the suppliers.  Instead, the payees or their authorized representatives need to appear to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer’s Office to claim the checks, therefore, acknowledge the receipt of the check payment in the Check Register.

We recommend that issuance of cash tickets to duly designated revenue collectors be properly monitored and controlled as well as their remittances/returns of consumed cash tickets in accordance with Section 30 of NGAS Manual Volume I.

We recommend that management require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).
We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer record all municipal transactions daily to establish accuracy of cash balances at any given period of time.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to update and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the general/subsidiary ledgers for these to be reconciled with the records of the disbursing officer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to hasten the application of fidelity bond required under Treasury Circular No. 02-009 to protect government funds from any unforeseen events.

Acquire and provide Biometric Machine to LGU personnel and have it installed near the temporary offices to register actual and prescribed office or work hours, as well as to safeguard the signature of the supervisor who will attest the correctness of the DTR of each employee.
Require submission of Daily Time Record (DTR) based on the biometric machine entries/records to support in computing the salaries and wages reflected in the payroll transactions of the municipality.
In the meantime, carefully monitor and scrutinize log book entries to arrive at the correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered by LGU personnel to avoid overpayment and waste of government funds.
We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the time frame.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18,  Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management observe the laws, rules and regulations governing the use of government funds to ensure optimum use of public funds for programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned employees to abide the above-cited provisions to give the Auditor the chance to a timely review and evaluation of the stipulations in the Contracts, PO/JO as to conduct inspection on deliveries of supplies and equipment;  give ample time for the Auditor to advise management on the defect, if any, in the contract for possible correction and to ascertain the existence of delivered items and compliance with specifications contained in the PO respectively.

1.8  We recommended Management  to  instruct  the  Accounting  Unit  and
Property/Supplies Unit to take actions on the following:

  a) Conduct physical inventory on all PPE owned by the LGU  and  submit
     the Report thereon to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section
     124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I;
  b) Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards  to  ensure  that
     all  discrepancies  are  immediately  investigated,   cleared   and
     reconciled with the books of accounts;
  c) Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the  duly  investigated
     reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances;
  d) Maintain complete and accurate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
     Cards (PPELC) for each class of equipment.

2.5   We recommended management to  stop  immediately  the  practice  of
including payments of loan  amortization  to  COEMCO  from  the  payroll
unless  the required net take home pay won’t  be  affected.  Henceforth,
strictly follow the provisions of law setting the net take home  pay  of
government employees.

3.5   We recommended to direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  stop  the
granting of honoraria to the members of Liga ng mga  Barangay.  Strictly
follow the guidelines on the granting of honoraria to  avoid  suspension
or disallowance in audit.

4.10  We recommended that management hold  its  seminars  and  trainings
within the territorial jurisdiction in accordance with  DILG  Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 to  avoid  the  incurrence  of
unnecessary and/or extravagant expenses.


4.11  We also recommended that the officials of the Municipality  shall,
in the preparation of programs and activities of the LGU, be  guided  by
the austerity measures that the government is promoting to preserve  its
scarce resources.

5.4  We recommended that management stop the  practice  of  using  other
funds for expenditures that are  not  supported  with  appropriation  to
avoid the depletion of the  affected  funds  which  may  result  to  the
delayed implementation of PPAs.


5.5  We also recommended for the Local Finance Committee and  the  Heads
of Offices to prepare budget proposals that will  answer  the  needs  of
their offices  to avoid using other funds.

6.5   We  recommended  to  indicate  in  all  purchase  orders  complete
information, such as mode of procurement, printed  name  of  the  person
signing the purchase order, date of receipt terms and condition, date of
delivery, etc., as required in COA Circular No. 96-010.

7.8  We recommended that management direct the Municipal  Accountant  to
thoroughly and accurately review the supporting  documents  attached  to
the claim/s to ensure the completeness of the documentation pursuant  to
Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.


7.9  We likewise  recommended  that  the  management  should  cause  the
submission  of  the  lacking  documents  of  the   transactions.   Other
supporting documents not thereto attached should be  submitted  to  this
Office.

8.4  We recommended management to adhere strictly to  the   DILG  &  DBM
Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.  2011-1  in  the  formulation  of   20%
Development Fund Plan to  fully  maximize  appropriations  for  the  20%
Development Fund.  We also recommend for the return  of  the  amount  of
P565,754.25 to the 20% Development Fund and to re-appropriate  the  same
for developmental projects.

9.6  We recommended that the Local School Board   strictly  comply  with
the provisions of Section 272 of RA7160  and  DECS-DBM  and  DILG  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 01–B dated June 25, 2001 in the  preparation  of
the SEF Budget to ensure the utilization of the same for the purpose  of
which is intended.

10.6   We recommended that management direct  the  GAD  Focal  Point  to
include in  the  submission  of  accomplishment  report,  the  documents
mentioned in Section 5 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

11.7  We recommended Local Chief Executive  and  other  concerned  to
appropriate in the LGUs Annual  Budget  one  percent  of  its  Internal
Revenue Allotment as required by RA 9344.

12.4  We recommended that management refrain from using government
funds for religious or private purposes and strictly comply with
Section 335 of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommend that management direct the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  Issue demand letters on unliquidated cash advances and remind grantees of cash advances on their responsibilities in the liquidation of cash advances within the required period.  Likewise, institute measures to enforce settlement of outstanding cash advances through deduction from the salary of the official or employee who fails to comply with the requirement on the liquidation of cash advances.

It is recommended that management adhere with the provisions of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of National Defense (DND), Governance Commission for GOCC (GCG) and the Commission on Audit (COA) Joint Memorandum Circular 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015 to ensure proper entitlement, release, use, reporting, and audit of Confidential Funds (CF) and Intelligence Funds (IF).

observations.recommendation: The municipal treasurer is enjoined to stop the practice of releasing salaries and wages to persons other that those named in the payroll.  If it is not possible for the employee concerned to receive his/her salary personally, a special power of attorney or letter of authorization sufficient in form and substance has to be required for the one who would receive in his stead by the cashier.  Exceptions to this are those persons cited earlier, but it should be properly established that the representative is an immediate family member of the family of the employee, or the officially designated liaison officer of the Office where the employee belong.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make reclassification entries to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRM to a special trust fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years pursuant to Par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 and Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommend that management utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly for programs and activities that support pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated by RA No. 10121 and promulgated by COA Circular No.2012-002.  We further recommend that the management submit an explanation for allowing the obligation of the LDRRMF for purposes other than those prescribed in the circular.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile records as to the barangays’ share in the prior year Real Property Taxes.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to release and/or remit immediately the full amount of P495,003.73 to the different barangays so that the same could be utilized for barangay development projects and to adhere to the prescribed period for releasing remitting the barangays’ share of real property taxes, which is five (5) days after the end of each quarter as prescribed under Article 362 of the Implementing Rules of RA 7160.

We recommend that management conduct an investigation and institute remedies in order to locate/recover the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents otherwise, the disallowance will become final and executory.
observations.managementAction: The disbursement vouchers remained unaccounted.  ICO-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Disbursing Officer filed an appeal on the issued disallowance.
observations.reason: The municipal accountant has drafted a letter to COA Central Office, however, she has a hard time in finding documents to support her letter, hence, this remained not submitted to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management cause the refund of the financial assistance to job order employees in the total amount of P273,000.00 for lack of legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a justification that these employees received the financial assistance because they are qualified for the AICS Program.
observations.reason: Documents subject to further verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting up of receivables from RPT and SET in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS Volume I and to avoid incurrence of negative balances at year end.  

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality on the negative balances of the Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income.
observations.managementAction: The account had been adjusted based on the amount given by the ICO-Municipal Treasurer.  However, the municipal account had not received a copy of the RPTAR to date.
observations.reason: No justification given.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the monthly financial reports of 2014 to the Office of the Auditor.  Henceforth, these reports should be submitted not later than the 20th day after the end of the month pursuant to NGAS Manual I.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and the related Reports of Disbursements/Reports of Checks Issued/Report of Collections and Deposits as well as Bank Reconciliation Statements immediately.  Henceforth, these should be submitted within 10 days from date of receipt of said documents from the Treasurer so that the Auditor could conduct the required review and verification of the transactions recorded in the books and render the necessary audit decisions on these transactions as well as validate the propriety of the account balances per books at any particular month or period.

Withhold the salary of the Municipal Accountant until such time that she shall have submitted the required reports pursuant to Section 41 (Par. 3) of PD1445.
observations.managementAction: Financial reports are not submitted on time.
observations.reason: Late transmittal of documents from the ICO-Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that issuance of cash tickets to duly designated revenue collectors be properly monitored and controlled as well as their remittances/returns of consumed cash tickets in accordance with Section 30 of NGAS Manual Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Cash tickets are not properly monitored.
observations.reason: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer overlook the monitoring of the Cash Tickets.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountable Officers concerned to immediately renew their bonds sufficient to cover their actual accountabilities required under Treasury Circular No. 02-009 and explanation from the Municipal Treasurer for her laxity to comply Section 305(f)  of Local Government Code of 1991 and Treasury Circular No. 02 -009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.reason: Note:  New ICO-Municipal Treasurer is not bonded as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill up or indicate all the data or information on the prescribed Driver’s Trip Ticket before traveling and to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

In the meantime, we recommend that management submit the reports subject of this AOM.

We recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman/ Secretariat to furnish the COA Office with copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Copies of POs are submitted in CY 2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

a.  Create a task force to identify the lots owned by the municipality and review if the same are titled in its name;

b.  Require the task force to work on the titling of all municipality-owned lots.

c.  Update/adjust the Land (201) account balance in the books of accounts based on the results of the identification of 
municipal-owned lots and its titling.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 

a.   Require the Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the Updated Description of Accounts under the New Government Accounting System so that transactions are accurately presented in the books. Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account should not include expenses for administrative or clerical services.

b.  Ensure strict adherence to the proper classification of personal services transactions in order not to circumvent the rule on personal services limitation as provided for in Section 325 (a) of the Local Government Code of 1991.

c.   Stop the practice of charging MOOE for personal services and observe strictly the Personal Services limitation.

We recommend that the management require coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: There are some department heads who are practicing the reimbursement method.  Urgency of the need to buy the supplies.
observations.reason: Urgency of the need to buy the supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the recipients refund the award paid by the municipality. Henceforth stop the granting of similar benefits to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: 3 of the Recipients of the award refunded.
observations.reason: Payroll for retrieval in the storage room.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality properly fill-up the Purchase Order and indicate therein the basic data as specified under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Late submission of POs.
observations.reason: Some data remained unindicated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances. Deduct all cash advances of staff who are still in service from any claim due them.

Stop the granting of additional cash advance/s to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommend that management:

Request the Municipal Mayor who is on leave of absence for the whereabouts of the reported missing disbursement vouchers and require the immediate submission of the same.

The municipal treasurer and accountant should coordinate, make a thorough search and trace in the logbook for the concerned personnel who had the last handled the disbursement vouchers to pinpoint responsibility.

The municipal treasurer should closely monitor the trail of the disbursement vouchers being the person accountable of the said documents.
observations.managementAction: In 2014, the allegedly missing checks increased to eighty-four (84)
observations.reason: The Former Municipal Mayor is out of the country.
